title: Repair Staking Pool Reward Accounting
summary: Repair a partially working staking pool that uses reward-per-share accounting, PDA vault custody, and per-user position PDAs.
businessLogic: An admin initializes a pool for one stake mint and one reward mint, with PDA-owned stake and reward vaults.
Users stake into the pool through position PDAs that track stake amount, pending rewards, and reward debt.
The admin deposits reward tokens into the reward vault, and rewards must be distributed proportionally across active stakers without erasing already-accrued rewards.

instructions.name: initialize
instructions.description: Create the pool configuration PDA, derive the pool authority PDA, and create the stake and reward vault token accounts bound to the configured mints.

instructions.name: deposit_rewards
instructions.description: Let the admin deposit reward tokens into the reward vault and update the global accumulated reward-per-share value only when there are active stakers.

instructions.name: stake
instructions.description: Allow a user to create or reuse a position PDA, preserve any already-earned rewards, and move stake tokens into the pool stake vault.

instructions.name: unstake
instructions.description: Allow only the position owner to withdraw stake from the pool while preserving already-accrued rewards and keeping pool totals correct.

instructions.name: claim
instructions.description: Allow only the position owner to claim exactly their pending rewards from the reward vault into their own reward token account.

invariants: Only the configured admin may deposit rewards, and reward deposits must fail when the pool has no active stakers.
Position PDAs must stay bound to exactly one pool and one owner.
Changing a user's stake amount after rewards have accrued must not erase the rewards they already earned.
Only the position owner may unstake or claim from that position.
Reward claims must transfer only the rewards actually owed, leaving any rounding dust in the reward vault.
failureConditions: Losing already-accrued rewards when a user stakes more or partially unstakes between reward epochs.
Allowing reward deposits to succeed when no users are staked.
Allowing an attacker to claim rewards or unstake funds from another user's position.
Allowing a position account to be used against the wrong pool without explicit validation.
